zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Maud VAUGHAN於大約1418年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾布利時，她的父親William DE VAUGHAN19歲，母親De Vaughan15歲。她於1468年與Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾布利結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1508年在她的家鄉享年91歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Maud VAUGHAN于大约1418年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾布利时，她的父亲William DE VAUGHAN19岁，母亲De Vaughan15岁。她于1468年与Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾布利结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1508年在她的家乡享年91岁。

nameMeanings.name: VAUGHAN
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

meanings: Possibly an altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .

variants.name: Vaughns
meanings: Welsh: variant of Vaughan , with English patronymic -s.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Maud VAUGHAN was born about 1418, in Burleigh, Gloucestershire, England, her father, William DE VAUGHAN, was 19 and her mother, De Vaughan, was 15. She married Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys in 1468, in Burleigh, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1508, in her hometown, at the age of 91.

Beaufort, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Maud VAUGHAN est née environ 1418, à Burleigh, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, William DE VAUGHAN, avait 19 ans et sa mère, De Vaughan, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys en 1468, à Burleigh, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1508, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 91 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Maud VAUGHAN um 1418 in Burleigh, Gloucestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William DE VAUGHAN 19 und ihre Mutter De Vaughan 15. Sie hat 1468 in Burleigh, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1508 im Alter von 91 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

Beaufort, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Maud VAUGHAN è nata il circa 1418 nel Burleigh, Gloucestershire, England, sua padre, William DE VAUGHAN, aveva 19 anni e sua madre, De Vaughan, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys nel 1468, nel Burleigh, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1508, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 91 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Maud VAUGHANが約1418年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブルレイグで生まれた時，父William DE VAUGHANは19歳，母De Vaughanは15歳であった。1468年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブルレイグでRichard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1508年に故郷で91歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Maud VAUGHAN(이)가 대략 1418년 영국 그루스스터실르 버리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William DE VAUGHAN(은)는 19세였고, 그녀의 어머니 De Vaughan(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1468년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 버리에서 Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1508년 그녀의 고향에서 91세의 나이로 사망했다.

Beaufort, Monmouthshire, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

Ela casou-se com Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys em 1468, em Burleigh, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Maud VAUGHAN родилась около 1418 г. в местности бурлиг, глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени William DE VAUGHAN было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали De Vaughan, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys в 1468 г. в местности бурлиг, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1508 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 91 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Maud VAUGHAN nació aproximadamente 1418, en Burleigh, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, William DE VAUGHAN, tenía 19 años y su mamá, De Vaughan, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys en 1468, en Burleigh, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1508, en su pueblo, a la edad de 91 años.
